goal: Honor the compact ?l= URL param in language detection on reading pages, matching what the URL serializer already emits.
done: getUrlLanguage in js/common.js now reads params.get('lang') || params.get('l') instead of lang only. Brings initShell's language lookup in line with parseReadingStateFromUrl and serializeReadingStateToUrl.
symptom: Reading pages opened with ?l=th rendered in English on first paint and the URL was being canonicalised back to ?l=en after initShell ran; live production confirmed rewriting ?l=th → ?l=en before fix.
root_cause: getUrlLanguage read only params.get('lang'), but serializeReadingStateToUrl in reading-url.js emits the compact l= form. initShell's unconditional state.currentLang assignment at common.js:669 then clobbered the value reading.js had already seeded from parseReadingStateFromUrl (reading.js:57,133), leaving ?l=-only URLs falling through to the path-based default.
fix: Added || params.get('l') to the URLSearchParams lookup in getUrlLanguage (js/common.js:566-571). Surgical one-function change; no other call sites touched.
verified: user-confirmed: live URL was rewriting ?l=th → ?l=en before fix
